provinces.name: Balochistan
provinces.facts: The largest province by area, rich in mineral resources.

provinces.name: Punjab
provinces.facts: The most populous province, known as the 'Land of Five Rivers'.

provinces.name: Sindh
provinces.facts: Home to the ancient Indus Valley Civilization and Karachi, the financial hub.

provinces.name: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
provinces.facts: Known for its mountainous terrain and the historic Khyber Pass.

provinces.name: Gilgit-Baltistan
provinces.facts: Host to five of the world's 14 'eight-thousanders', including K2.

provinces.name: Azad Jammu and Kashmir
provinces.facts: A self-governing region known for its breathtaking natural beauty.

regions.name: Punjab Core
regions.description: Primary wheat belt of Punjab.

regions.name: Upper Sindh
regions.description: High-yield districts like Sukkur and Ghotki.

regions.name: Peshawar Valley
regions.description: Fertile plains of KP.

facts.Summary: Backbone of national food security, grown in Rabi season.
facts.Climate: Cool season (10-25°C), requires dry weather for ripening.
facts.Soil: Fertile Alluvial or Loamy soil of Indus Plains.
facts.Irrigation: Linked to extensive canal networks and barrages.

regions.name: Kalar Tract
regions.description: Famous Basmati rice belt between Ravi and Chenab rivers.

regions.name: Upper Sindh Rice Belt
regions.description: Larkana, Jacobabad, and Shikarpur districts.

regions.name: Nasirabad Division
regions.description: Balochistan's primary rice area.

facts.Summary: Major Kharif cash crop and key export (Basmati).
facts.Soil: Heavy clay loam or silt loam with high water retention.
facts.Water: Water-intensive, roots must remain submerged (40+ inches rainfall equivalent).
facts.Irrigation: Heavily dependent on perennial canal water.

regions.name: Central KP Belt
regions.description: Mardan, Swabi, and Peshawar districts.

regions.name: East Punjab
regions.description: Sahiwal and Okara districts.

facts.Summary: Important for food, livestock feed, and industrial starch.
facts.Climate: Warm weather, sensitive to frost and drought.
facts.Water: 22-27 inches, requires regular and consistent irrigation.

regions.name: Central Punjab
regions.description: Faisalabad, Sargodha, and Jhang.

regions.name: Sindh Sugarcane Belt
regions.description: Badin, Thatta, and Hyderabad.

regions.name: Peshawar-Mardan Plain
regions.description: Major KP production area.

facts.Summary: High-value cash crop for the processing/sugar industry.
facts.Soil: Deep Alluvial soils with good drainage.
facts.Water: High water requirement, 10-15 month long growing cycle.

regions.name: South Punjab Triangle
regions.description: Multan, Bahawalpur, and R.Y. Khan - 70% of national output.

regions.name: Lower Sindh Belt
regions.description: Sanghar and Hyderabad districts.

facts.Summary: Silver fiber of Pakistan, major foreign exchange earner.
facts.Climate: Long hot summer, harvesting in dry autumn.

regions.name: Thal Desert Pulse Belt
regions.description: Bhakkar and Layyah - major chickpea production.

facts.Summary: Essential source of protein for the local population.
facts.Soil: Grown on sandy soils with minimal water.

regions.name: Potohar Belt
regions.description: Sunflower and Mustard in rain-fed regions.

regions.name: Lower Indus Plain
regions.description: Sunflower hubs in Sindh.

facts.Summary: Crops for edible oil (Sunflower, Mustard, Canola).
facts.Economic: Crucial to reduce oil import dependency.

livestock.name: Buffalo Farming Regions

regions.name: Central Punjab Plain
regions.description: High concentration dairy production near urban centers.

regions.name: Canal Colonies
regions.description: Irrigated districts in Punjab.

livestock.facts: Punjab is the main buffalo breeding province. Specific breeds include Nili-Ravi and Kundi. Key districts: Sahiwal, Okara, Faisalabad.

livestock.name: Sheep and Goat Farming

regions.name: Balochistan Uplands
regions.description: Nomadic herding in rugged terrains.

regions.name: Tharparkar (Sindh)
regions.description: Thari breeds famous for endurance.

regions.name: Thal & Cholistan Deserts
regions.description: Traditional desert grazing.

livestock.facts: Mainly found in arid and semi-arid regions. Sheep are raised for wool and meat; goats are 'the poor man's cow' providing milk and meat.

regions.name: Multan Belt
regions.description: Chaunsa and Anwar Ratol hub.

regions.name: Mirpurkhas Belt
regions.description: Famous Sindhri mango orchards.

facts.Summary: King of Fruits, major export earner.
facts.Water: Moderate during flowering, heavy during fruiting.

regions.name: Sargodha-Bhalwal Belt
regions.description: 95% of world's Kinnow production hub.

facts.Summary: World-leading producer of the Kinnow variety.
facts.Climate: Winter chilling required for color and sugar.
facts.Soil: Medium to light Texture Sandy Loam.
facts.Water: Sensitive to waterlogging, needs regular irrigation.

regions.name: Quetta-Ziarat Belt
regions.description: High-altitude orchards in Balochistan.

regions.name: GB Valleys
regions.description: Organic apple production in Northern valleys.

facts.Summary: Temperate fruit grown in high-altitude regions.
facts.Climate: Cool climate, requires significant winter chilling.
facts.Soil: Loamy soil with rich organic matter.

regions.name: Kasur District
regions.description: One of the largest man-made forests in the world.

facts.Significance: Man-made forest originally developed to provide fuel-wood for locomotives during the British Raj.

regions.name: High Northern Zone
regions.description: Highest altitude tree lines in GB.

facts.Role: Crucial for high-altitude biodiversity and soil stability.

regions.name: Swat-Kaghan Ranges
regions.description: Main timber forests of the North.

facts.Role: Main source of timber and massive carbon sink.

regions.name: Murree Hills
regions.description: Natural Chir Pine zone in Murree tehsil.

regions.name: Abbottabad Foothills
regions.description: Subtropical chir pine zone of Abbottabad.

facts.Climate: Dry with hot summers and cold winters.

regions.name: Punjab Thorn Plains
regions.description: Sialkot, Gujrat, and Jhelum plains.

regions.name: Potohar Foothills
regions.description: Dry deciduous forests in Rawalpindi foothills.

facts.Role: Xerophytic vegetation adapted to water scarcity.

regions.name: Indus River Banks
regions.description: Narrow belts along the Indus River floodplains.

facts.Location: Indus floodplains in Sindh and Punjab.
facts.Role: Flood mitigation and protecting riverbanks from erosion.

regions.name: Indus Delta
regions.description: Salt-tolerant coastal ecosystems.

facts.Role: Coastal defense against cyclones and fish nurseries.

regions.name: Heavy Intensity Zone (Northern)
regions.description: Footshills of Northern Mountains (Murree, Kaghan) receiving over 1000mm.

regions.name: Moderate Intensity Zone (Central)
regions.description: Central Punjab plains receiving 500mm-1000mm.

regions.name: Light Intensity Zone (South)
regions.description: Southern Punjab and Sindh receiving less than 500mm.

facts.Origin: Bay of Bengal and Arabian Sea
facts.Importance: Crucial for Kharif crops like rice and cotton.

regions.name: Heavy Snow/Rain Zone (Mountainous)
regions.description: High altitude northern and western mountains.

regions.name: Moderate Rain Zone (Plateau)
regions.description: Balochistan Plateau and surroundings.

facts.Importance: Vital for Rabi crops like wheat.

regions.name: High Frequency Zone
regions.description: Northern and Central plains experiencing local heating.

facts.Mechanism: Intense land heating leads to rising air and thunder clouds.
facts.Nature: Short, intense bursts often accompanied by hail.

landforms.name: Doabs of Punjab

regions.name: Sind Sagar Doab
regions.description: Land between Indus and Jhelum rivers.

regions.name: Chaj (Jech) Doab
regions.description: Land between Jhelum and Chenab rivers.

regions.name: Rechna Doab
regions.description: Land between Chenab and Ravi rivers.

regions.name: Bari Doab
regions.description: Land between Ravi and Sutlej rivers.

landforms.facts: Fertile tracts of land between major rivers, heart of Pakistan's agriculture.

landforms.name: Plains & Deltas

regions.name: Piedmont Plains
regions.description: Plains at the foothills of Sulaiman and Kirthar ranges (Derajat).

regions.name: Indus Tidal Delta
regions.description: Triangular landform where Indus meets the Arabian Sea.

regions.name: Active Flood Plains
regions.description: Narrow strip along rivers flooded almost every year.

landforms.facts: Diverse physical features essential for geography studies.

ports.name: Karachi Port
ports.facts: Pakistan's premier commercial gateway and oldest port.

ports.name: Port Qasim
ports.facts: Pakistan's second busiest port, handles industrial cargo.

ports.name: Gwadar Port
ports.facts: Strategic deep-sea port centerpiece of CPEC.

ports.name: Port of Ormara
ports.facts: Naval and commercial port in Balochistan.

ports.name: Port of Pasni
ports.facts: Important fishing and commercial hub in Balochistan.

ports.name: Port of Jiwani
ports.facts: Strategic port near the Iranian border.

ports.name: Keti Bandar
ports.facts: Historical port city on the Indus Delta.

ports.name: Lahore Dry Port
ports.facts: First dry port of Pakistan, established in 1973.

ports.name: Sialkot Dry Port
ports.facts: Private sector dry port supporting the export industry.

ports.name: Peshawar Dry Port
ports.facts: Key inland port for transit trade with Afghanistan.

ports.name: Quetta Dry Port
ports.facts: Inland gateway for trade via Balochistan.

ports.name: Multan Dry Port
ports.facts: Serving the agricultural and industrial hub of South Punjab.

ports.name: Faisalabad Dry Port
ports.facts: Supports the massive textile export industry.

ports.name: Sust Dry Port
ports.facts: Highest dry port, gateway for trade with China.

rivers.name: Indus River
rivers.facts: Lifeline of Pakistan, flowing from the Himalayas (near Mansarovar) through the entire length of the country to the Arabian Sea.

rivers.name: Jhelum River
rivers.facts: Westernmost of the five Punjab rivers, known for Mangla Dam and its confluence with Chenab at Trimmu.

rivers.name: Chenab River
rivers.facts: Largest of the Punjab rivers by volume, merges with Jhelum at Trimmu and later forms the Panjnad.

rivers.name: Ravi River
rivers.facts: Flows past Lahore, essential for central Punjab's irrigation; merges with Chenab below Sidhuai.

rivers.name: Sutlej River
rivers.facts: Longest of the Punjab rivers, enters from India near Ganda Singh Wala and merges with Chenab to form Panjnad.

rivers.name: Kabul River
rivers.facts: Major western tributary of the Indus, originating in Afghanistan and entering Indus at Attock.

rivers.name: Swat River
rivers.facts: Starts from Ushu and Utran glaceries, flows through Swat Valley, and joins Kabul River near Nisatta.

rivers.name: Kurram River
rivers.facts: A key western river flowing through Kurram Agency and Bannu, entering the Indus near Isa Khel.

rivers.name: Gomal River
rivers.facts: Drains the Gomal Pass and South Waziristan; its water is stored in the Gomal Zam Dam for irrigation.

rivers.name: Zhob River
rivers.facts: Located in northern Balochistan, it flows into the Gomal River near Khajuri Kach.

rivers.name: Hingol River
rivers.facts: Longest river of Balochistan, famous for the Hingol National Park and the Makran coast.

rivers.name: Dasht River
rivers.facts: Crucial for Kech and Gawadar districts, the Mirani Dam is built on this river.

barrages.name: Sukkur Barrage
barrages.facts: The pride of Pakistan's irrigation system, the largest barrage by canal output.

barrages.name: Kotri Barrage
barrages.facts: Also known as Ghulam Muhammad Barrage, serves lower Sindh.

barrages.name: Guddu Barrage
barrages.facts: Supplies water to northern Sindh and parts of Balochistan.

barrages.name: Taunsa Barrage
barrages.facts: Major barrage in Punjab supporting DG Khan and Muzaffargarh.

barrages.name: Chashma Barrage
barrages.facts: Integral for the Chashma-Jhelum link canal.

barrages.name: Jinnah Barrage
barrages.facts: Provides irrigation to the Thal desert region.

barrages.name: Trimmu Barrage
barrages.facts: At the confluence of Jhelum and Chenab rivers.

barrages.name: Panjnad Barrage
barrages.facts: Where all five Punjab rivers finally unite before joining Indus.

barrages.name: Marala Headworks
barrages.facts: A massive headworks near Sialkot providing water to major link canals.

barrages.name: Rasul Barrage
barrages.facts: Connects Jhelum and Chenab via the Rasul-Qadirabad link canal.

barrages.name: Khanki Headworks
barrages.facts: One of the oldest headworks, recently upgraded.

barrages.name: Sidhnai Headworks
barrages.facts: Irrigates the lower Bari Doab region.

barrages.name: Balloki Headworks
barrages.facts: Crucial for the Balloki-Sulemanki link canal.

barrages.name: Sulemanki Headworks
barrages.facts: Supplies water to the Fordwah and Eastern Sadiqia canals.

barrages.name: Islam Barrage
barrages.facts: Serving the Bahawalpur region irrigation needs.

dams.name: Tarbela Dam
dams.facts: Largest earth-filled dam in the world, on Indus River.

dams.name: Mangla Dam
dams.facts: Ninth largest dam globally, on Jhelum River.

dams.name: Mirani Dam
dams.facts: Large dam in Balochistan for irrigation and water supply.

dams.name: Misriot Dam
dams.facts: Small irrigation dam near Rawalpindi, Punjab.

dams.name: Satpara Dam
dams.facts: Medium-sized dam providing water and power to Skardu, GB.

dams.name: Sabakzai Dam
dams.facts: Small dam on Sawar Rud, Balochistan.

dams.name: Akra Kaur Dam
dams.facts: Primary water source for Gwadar, Balochistan.

dams.name: Chashma Akhora Khel
dams.facts: Small dam in District Karak, KPK.

dams.name: Sarozai Dam
dams.facts: Irrigation and water supply dam in District Hangu, KPK.

dams.name: Darawat Dam
dams.facts: Small dam across Nai Baran River, Sindh.

dams.name: Dhok Talian Dam
dams.facts: Small irrigation dam in District Chakwal, Punjab.

dams.name: Tain Pura Dam
dams.facts: Small dam providing irrigation in Tehsil Dina, Jhelum.

dams.name: Shahpur Dam
dams.facts: Important small dam for Rawalpindi/Attock irrigation.

industries.name: Pakistan Steel Mills
industries.facts: Largest industrial complex in Pakistan, built with Soviet assistance.

industries.name: Fertilizer Plants
industries.facts: Vital for agriculture, primarily located near gas fields for raw material.

industries.name: Cement Industry
industries.facts: Located near limestone and gypsum deposits in the Salt Range and Western Hills.

industries.name: Sugar Industries
industries.facts: Located in sugarcane growing belts of Punjab, Sindh, and KP.

industries.name: Cotton Textile Industries
industries.facts: Pakistan's largest industrial sector, focused on spinning, weaving, and garments.

energy_pipelines.name: Sui-Karachi Gas Pipeline
energy_pipelines.facts: Transports natural gas from Sui field to Karachi hub.

energy_pipelines.name: Parco Oil Pipeline
energy_pipelines.facts: White oil pipeline system from Karachi to Mahmood Kot (Multan).

airports.name: International Airports
airports.facts: Major gateways for international trade and travel.

airports.name: Domestic Airports
airports.facts: Crucial for connectivity in mountainous and remote regions.

population.name: Densely Populated Areas
population.facts: Areas with over 600 persons per sq km, primarily urban/industrial centers.

population.name: Sparsely Populated Areas
population.facts: Areas with less than 20 persons per sq km due to arid climate and rugged terrain.

minerals.name: Limestone

locations.name: Punjab Salt Range
locations.description: Huge deposits in Chakwal and Mianwali.

minerals.uses: Chief raw material for Cement industries; also used in Glass, Paper, and Iron smelting.
minerals.significance: Cornerstone of Pakistan's industrial economy, supporting the massive construction sector.

infrastructure.name: 500kV Main Transmission Corridor
infrastructure.facts: The primary high-voltage North-South backbone connecting Tarbela and Mangla power houses to the load centers in Punjab and Sindh.

infrastructure.name: Matiari-Lahore HVDC Line
infrastructure.facts: The first High Voltage Direct Current (HVDC) transmission line in Pakistan, built under CPEC to transmit power from South to North.

infrastructure.name: Hub-Karachi Transmission Link
infrastructure.facts: Connects the Hub Power Plant to the Karachi K-Electric grid.

infrastructure.name: CASA-1000 Corridor (Planned)
infrastructure.facts: Central Asia-South Asia power project to bring clean energy from Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan to Pakistan via Afghanistan.

industrial_zones.name: SITE Industrial Area
industrial_zones.facts: Pakistan's largest and oldest industrial estate, home to hundreds of textile, chemical, and food processing units.

industrial_zones.name: Korangi Industrial Area
industrial_zones.facts: Major hub for leather, textiles, and pharmaceuticals in Karachi.

industrial_zones.name: Bin Qasim Industrial Park
industrial_zones.facts: Special Economic Zone catering to engineering, chemicals, and heavy industries near Port Qasim.

industrial_zones.name: Sundar Industrial Estate
industrial_zones.facts: Most modern industrial estate in Punjab, featuring diverse industries from electronics to consumer goods.

industrial_zones.name: M-3 Industrial City
industrial_zones.facts: Largest Special Economic Zone in Pakistan, located in Faisalabad, focusing on textile, garments, and pharmaceuticals.

industrial_zones.name: Hattar Industrial Estate
industrial_zones.facts: Strategically located at the junction of KP and Punjab, hosting chemical, ghee, and beverage industries.

industrial_zones.name: Gadoon Amazai Industrial Estate
industrial_zones.facts: Located in Swabi, KP, it flourished as a major textile and plastic manufacturing hub of the region.

industrial_zones.name: Rashakai Special Economic Zone
industrial_zones.facts: Priority CPEC project in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, aimed at processing minerals, textiles, and light engineering.

industrial_zones.name: Gwadar Free Zone
industrial_zones.facts: Centerpiece of CPEC, facilitating transshipment and manufacturing for the Middle East and African markets.

industrial_zones.name: Sialkot Export Processing Zone
industrial_zones.facts: Globally recognized for sports goods, surgical instruments, and leather jackets manufacturing and export.

industrial_zones.name: Risalpur Export Processing Zone
industrial_zones.facts: Facilitating export-oriented industries in the KP region, particularly in gemstones and food processing.

industrial_zones.name: Hayatabad Industrial Estate
industrial_zones.facts: The premier industrial zone of Peshawar, serving local demand and trade with Afghanistan.

industrial_zones.name: Dhabeji Special Economic Zone
industrial_zones.facts: A major CPEC project in Sindh, located near Port Qasim, ideal for heavy industry and exports.

industrial_zones.name: Bostan Special Economic Zone
industrial_zones.facts: CPEC project in Balochistan, focused on agriculture processing and livestock-related industries.

mountain_ranges.name: Karakoram Range

passes.name: Khunjerab Pass
passes.facts: World's highest paved international border crossing, part of the Karakoram Highway.

mountain_ranges.facts: Host to 60+ peaks over 7,000m. It is a mass of lofty mountains and massive glaciers like Baltoro. Known for its rugged terrain and extreme cold.

mountain_ranges.name: Himalayan Range

passes.name: Babusar Pass
passes.facts: Highest point in Kaghan Valley; a major seasonal road link.

mountain_ranges.facts: Part of the world's highest mountain system. Marks the western anchor of the Great Himalayas in Pakistan. Nanga Parbat is its crowning peak.

mountain_ranges.name: Hindu Kush Range

passes.name: Lowari Pass
passes.facts: Strategic pass connecting Chitral; now supplemented by the Lowari Tunnel.

mountain_ranges.facts: Extends from central Afghanistan into northwestern Pakistan. Tirich Mir is the highest point outside the Himalaya-Karakoram system.

mountain_ranges.name: Safed Koh (White Mountain)
mountain_ranges.facts: Located south of the Hindu Kush, it forms a natural wall between Pakistan and Afghanistan.

mountain_ranges.name: Sulaiman Range
mountain_ranges.facts: Forms the eastern edge of the Iranian plateau. Known for its historical significance and diverse flora.

mountain_ranges.name: Waziristan Hills
mountain_ranges.facts: Rough, rugged hills located in the southern part of the Northwestern Highlands. They are home to the strategic Gomal Pass.

mountain_ranges.name: Kirthar Range
mountain_ranges.facts: Delineates the boundary between Sindh and Balochistan. Features Gorakh Hill.

deserts.name: Thar Desert
deserts.facts: Pakistan's largest desert, located in Sindh. Known for its unique culture, monsoon-dependent flora, and the Thar Coal reserves.

deserts.name: Cholistan Desert
deserts.facts: Locally known as Rohi, this desert in Bahawalpur is part of the Great Indian Desert. Features the historic Derawar Fort.

deserts.name: Thal Desert
deserts.facts: Located in the Doab between the Indus and Jhelum/Chenab rivers. Partially reclaimed for agriculture through the Thal Canal project.

deserts.name: Kharan Desert
deserts.facts: A sandy and mountainous desert in Balochistan. It is one of the most arid regions in Pakistan, characterized by dry basins (Hamuns).

plateaus.name: Potwar Plateau
plateaus.facts: A rugged landscape in northern Punjab. Rich in minerals like salt, gypsum, and coal. Home to the Soan River and salt range.

plateaus.name: Balochistan Plateau
plateaus.facts: A vast, high-altitude arid plateau. Characterized by 'Karez' irrigation, mineral wealth, and extreme temperature variations.

mines.name: Khewra Salt Mine
mines.facts: World's second-largest salt mine, located in the Salt Range, Punjab.

mines.name: Saindak Copper-Gold Mine
mines.facts: Major copper and gold deposit in Chagai District, Balochistan.

mines.name: Reko Diq Mine
mines.facts: One of the world's largest undeveloped copper and gold deposits.

mines.name: Thar Coal Mine
mines.facts: Contains 175 billion tons of lignite coal, one of the largest reserves in the world.

mines.name: Kalabagh Iron Ore Mine
mines.facts: Significant iron ore deposits located near Mianwali, Punjab.

mines.name: Muslim Bagh Chromite Mine
mines.facts: Primary source of high-grade chromite in Pakistan, located in Balochistan.

mining_methods.name: Open-Cast Mining
mining_methods.facts: Surface mining where overburden is removed to reach minerals.

mining_methods.name: Adit Mining
mining_methods.facts: Horizontal tunnels bored into hillsides to reach coal/minerals.

mining_methods.name: Shaft Mining
mining_methods.facts: Vertical tunnels sunk deep into the ground for deep-seated minerals.

energy_resources.name: Tarbela Dam (HEP)
energy_resources.facts: Largest earth-filled dam in the world, primary source of HEP.

energy_resources.name: Mangla Dam (HEP)
energy_resources.facts: Second largest HEP station on Jhelum River.

energy_resources.name: Chashma Nuclear
energy_resources.facts: Major nuclear power complex in Punjab.

energy_resources.name: K-Nuclear (KANUPP)
energy_resources.facts: Coastal nuclear power plant serving Karachi.

energy_resources.name: Guddu Thermal
energy_resources.facts: One of the largest thermal power stations in Sindh.

energy_resources.name: Jamshoro Thermal
energy_resources.facts: Oil and coal fired plant in Lower Sindh.

energy_resources.name: Sui Gas Field
energy_resources.facts: Pakistan's largest gas field, discovered in 1952. It revolutionized the energy landscape of the country.

energy_resources.name: Mari Gas Field
energy_resources.facts: One of the largest gas fields in Pakistan, located in Ghotki District, Sindh.

energy_resources.name: Qadirpur Gas Field
energy_resources.facts: Significant gas producer in Sindh, operated by OGDCL.

energy_resources.name: Dhurnal Oil Field
energy_resources.facts: Major oil field in the Potwar Basin, Punjab.

energy_resources.name: Tut Oil Field
energy_resources.facts: Pioneer oil field discovered in the 1960s in Punjab.

energy_resources.name: Uch Gas Field
energy_resources.facts: Feeds the Uch Power Plant, providing essential electricity to the national grid.

energy_resources.name: Pirkoh Gas Field
energy_resources.facts: Located in the Sulaiman Range, Balochistan.

energy_resources.name: Dhodak Oil & Gas Field
energy_resources.facts: Important field in Dera Ghazi Khan, Punjab.

roads.name: M-1 Motorway (Peshawar-Islamabad)
roads.facts: Connects Peshawar to Islamabad, a vital link between KP and the capital.

roads.name: M-2 Motorway (Islamabad-Lahore)
roads.facts: The first motorway in South Asia, connecting the capital to the heart of Punjab.

roads.name: M-3 Motorway (Lahore-Abdul Hakeem)
roads.facts: Provides a direct link between Lahore and Multan.

roads.name: M-4 Motorway (Pindi Bhattian-Multan)
roads.facts: Connects the industrial hub Faisalabad to Multan.

roads.name: M-5 Motorway (Multan-Sukkur)
roads.facts: The longest motorway in Pakistan, a key part of CPEC.

roads.name: M-6 Motorway (Sukkur-Hyderabad)
roads.facts: Planned motorway to connect Sukkur and Hyderabad.

roads.name: M-8 Motorway (Ratodero-Gwadar)
roads.facts: Strategically connects Sindh to the deep-sea port of Gwadar.

roads.name: M-9 Motorway (Hyderabad-Karachi)
roads.facts: Critical link between Karachi and the rest of the country.

roads.name: M-15 Motorway (Hazara Motorway)
roads.facts: Enhances connectivity in the Hazara region of KP.

trade_routes.name: CPEC Western Alignment
trade_routes.facts: Shortest route via D.I. Khan and Quetta, developing western regions.

trade_routes.name: CPEC Eastern Alignment
trade_routes.facts: Route via Lahore and Sukkur, utilizing existing motorway networks.

trade_routes.name: CPEC Central Alignment
trade_routes.facts: Planned alignment through the heart of the country.

trade_routes.name: Grand Trunk (GT) Road (N-5)
trade_routes.facts: The historic lifeline of the subcontinent, the longest national highway.

trade_routes.name: Indus Highway (N-55)
trade_routes.facts: Connects Karachi to Peshawar via the western bank of the Indus River.

trade_routes.name: Karakoram Highway (N-35)
trade_routes.facts: The 'Eighth Wonder of the World', connecting Pakistan to China.

trade_routes.name: Makran Coastal Highway (N-10)
trade_routes.facts: Scenic route along the Arabian Sea connecting Karachi to Gwadar.

railways.name: Main Line 1 (ML-1) Karachi-Peshawar
railways.facts: The primary railway artery of Pakistan, scheduled for major CPEC upgrade.

fish_farms.name: Inland Fish Farms

regions.name: Thatta & Badin
regions.description: Major cluster of commercial fish ponds in Sindh.

fish_farms.facts: Commercial aquaculture focuses on species like Rohu, Mori, and Thaila.

drought_areas.name: Tharparkar Drought Zone
drought_areas.facts: Chronically drought-prone area with low rainfall and water scarcity.

mountain_passes.name: Khyber Pass
mountain_passes.facts: Historic gateway through the Safed Koh mountains, connecting Central Asia to the Indian subcontinent.

mountain_passes.name: Bolan Pass
mountain_passes.facts: Pass through the Toba Kakar range connecting the Indus valley to Quetta; vital for trade and military history.
